title: Senior Product Marketing Manager; AI & Product Developer Marketing
company_focus:
AI-powered digital analytics platform for product
development
description:
Amplitude is the leading AI analytics platform, helping over
4,300 customers—including Atlassian, Burger King,
NBCUniversal, Square, and Under Armour—build better products
and digital experiences. With powerful AI Agents embedded
across our platform, teams can analyze, test, and optimize
user experiences faster than ever. Ranked #1 across multiple
categories by G2, Amplitude is the best-in-class solution
for product, data, and marketing teams. Learn more at
amplitude.com.

As an organization, we deliver for our customers by living
our values. We operate from a place of humility, take
ownership of problems and successes, approach challenges
with a growth mindset, and put our customers at the center
of everything we do.

Amplitude’s Commitment to Diversity Equity & Inclusion
(DEI): Amplitude believes that diversity enables the
creation of better products, improves the ability to solve
complex problems, and drives more powerful solutions. We
strive to create an environment of inclusion—one focused on
psychological safety, empathy, and human connection—that
will allow employees of all backgrounds to thrive.

Product development is fundamentally being transformed by
AI. As coding gets dramatically faster, the critical
bottlenecks shift: What should we build? Which AI model
drives the outcomes we care about? Did this feature actually
drive the outcomes we care about?  This shift is creating a
new software development lifecycle in which analytics plays
a mission critical role where Product Managers have the
autonomy to test hypotheses, measure AI model performance,
and iterate based on real outcomes.

Amplitude is building the platform that enables this new
reality— Our experimentation and analytics stack gives PMs
the tools to work more effectively with engineering: faster
feedback loops, clearer impact measurement, and the
confidence to make bold product bets.

About the role

As a Sr. Product Marketing Manager, you will: partner with
our product, GTM, and customer teams to position Amplitude
as the platform that democratizes data-driven product
development—empowering PMs to act with speed and conviction
in the AI era.

We're looking for a Sr. Product Marketing Manager to
position Amplitude as the platform that empowers product
builders to ship, test, and optimize AI products with
confidence. You'll own go-to-market strategy for our Feature
Experimentation, Feature Flags, SDKs and LLM Analytics—the
stack that enables Product teams to move faster, experiment
fearlessly, and make decisions based on real user outcomes,
not gut instinct.

This is a high-impact role for someone who understands both
product development workflows and has the technical fluency
to bridge Product and Engineering teams. You'll help PMs
gain the autonomy to test ideas, validate hypotheses, and
drive product velocity without constant engineering
dependencies.

What you will own

Shape the narrative for product development

- Articulate why analytics becomes exponentially more critical
  as AI accelerates product development—when you can build
  10x faster, knowing what to build and whether it worked
  becomes the competitive edge
- Position Amplitude as the platform that gives Product teams
  the autonomy to test AI models, validate features, and
  drive outcomes without engineering bottlenecks
- Tell the story of how experimentation culture transforms
  product velocity—from "let's discuss what might work" to
  "let's test and know what works"

Bridge Product and Engineering with authentic fluency

- Develop messaging that resonates with Product Managers while
  demonstrating enough technical depth to earn credibility
  with Engineering partners
- Create content that helps PMs understand how to leverage
  experimentation, feature flags, and analytics throughout
  the product lifecycle—not just at launch
- Speak comfortably about product development workflows:
  hypothesis testing, gradual rollouts, AI model evaluation,
  conversion optimization, and impact measurement

Drive adoption across product organizations

- Lead launches for experimentation, feature management, and
  LLM analytics capabilities with a focus on PM workflows
  and time-to-value
- Build enablement programs that help sales and customer
  success teams speak to product organizations about
  experimentation culture, analytics maturity, and AI-
  readiness
- Partner with product to shape roadmap based on how modern
  product teams want to work—faster feedback loops, clearer
  impact visibility, more autonomy

Build compelling product stories with customer evidence

- Work with customers building AI products to showcase how
  experimentation enables product velocity at scale
- Create case studies that demonstrate real outcomes:
  increases in user satisfaction, lifts in conversion,
  ability to safely test models on small cohorts
- Translate "we measure real things instead of fake things"
  into repeatable playbooks for product teams across
  different stages and verticals

Position Amplitude's unique value for product teams

- Differentiate Amplitude from developer-first tools by
  emphasizing how we empower Product teams with analytics
  autonomy, not just engineers
- Identify gaps where product teams struggle to get fast,
  actionable insights from their experimentation and
  analytics tools
- Articulate why analytics is becoming more load-bearing as AI
  makes building faster but deciding what to build harder

What we are looking for:

- 6+ years of product marketing experience in B2B SaaS,
  ideally with exposure to product development tools,
  experimentation platforms, or analytics products
- Product development fluency: You understand modern product
  workflows—how teams prioritize, ship, measure, and
  iterate. You can speak credibly about experimentation
  methodologies, hypothesis testing, and data-driven
  decision making
- Ability to bridge Product and Engineering: You're technical
  enough to understand how things work under the hood and
  can translate between PM priorities and engineering
  constraints. You don't need to write code, but you
  understand the product development lifecycle
- Experience with experimentation or analytics tools: Hands-on
  understanding of A/B testing, feature flags, or product
  analytics—ideally through direct use or through marketing
  these capabilities
- Exceptional storytelling: Ability to craft narratives that
  resonate with Product leaders—connecting product velocity,
  experimentation culture, and business outcomes in
  compelling ways
- Cross-functional leadership: Proven ability to partner
  effectively with product, sales, customer success, and
  marketing teams to drive launches and adoption

Preferred Qualifications:

- Hands-on experience with digital analytics and Amplitude a
  plus
- Savvy with AI tools and a desire to readily leverage these
  tools to scale.
- Familiarity with analytics tools such as Amplitude or
  similar platforms.
- Solid technical chops and an ability to quickly dive in and
  learn new technologies.
- Hands-on experience with customers and full-funnel
  marketing.

What makes you successful in this role:

- Product empathy: You understand what PMs care about
  (velocity, autonomy, impact visibility, confidence in
  decisions) and can authentically speak to those priorities
- Technical curiosity: You ask good questions, learn quickly,
  and can explain technical concepts to non-technical
  audiences without oversimplifying
- Comfort with ambiguity: AI-native product development is
  still emerging. You're energized by shaping narratives in
  new categories rather than executing established playbooks
- Bias toward action: You ship, iterate, and leverage AI tools
  to move faster. You test hypotheses with real customers
  and refine based on feedback
- Experimentation mindset: You practice what you preach—using
  data to inform decisions, running tests when you're
  uncertain, and measuring what matters

Why This Role Matters

The next decade of product development will be defined by
teams who can:

- Ship 10x faster with AI, making "what to build" and "did it
  work" the only sustainable competitive advantages
- Test AI models continuously—not just at launch—to find the
  ones that actually drive user satisfaction and business
  outcomes
- Make decisions based on real user behavior and business
  metrics, not proxies like clicks or vanity metrics
- Give Product Managers the autonomy to experiment, measure,
  and iterate without constant engineering dependencies

Product teams building AI products face unprecedented
complexity: unpredictable model outputs, variable costs,
rapidly evolving capabilities, and the pressure to ship
faster than ever. The winners will be the teams who can
experiment fearlessly, learn quickly, and make data-driven
decisions at velocity.

This role sits at the center of that transformation. You'll
help define how the industry thinks about product analytics
in the age of AI, position Amplitude as the platform that
enables faster, more autonomous product teams, and work with
cutting-edge customers who are redefining what's possible.

If you want to shape how product teams build and optimize AI
products—and have the product development fluency to speak
credibly to this audience—this is the role for you.

Who We Are

The Company: Amplitude is filled with humble, life-long
learners who are eager to help one another and the company
succeed. Our values of growth mindset, ownership, and
humility are core to the way we work: we’re tenacious in the
face of challenges, we take the initiative to solve problems
that drive our shared success, and we operate from a place
of empathy and openness, seeking to understand many points
of view.

The Product: Amplitude is a digital analytics platform—we
help companies capture data they can trust, uncover clear
insights about customer behavior, and take faster action.
This empowers teams to build better product experiences that
drive business growth. We’re super proud of what we’ve built
and continue to expand: a platform that empowers companies
to thrive in the digital era.

We care about the well-being of our team: We offer
competitive pay and benefits packages that reflect our
commitment to the health and well-being of our Ampliteers.

Some of our benefit programs include:

- Excellent Medical, Dental and Vision insurance coverages,
  with 100% employer-paid premiums for employee Medical,
  Dental, Vision on select plans
- Flexible time off, paid holidays, and more
- Generous stipends to spend on what matters most to you,
  whether that’s wellness (monthly), commuter
  transit/parking (monthly), learning and development
  (quarterly), home office equipment (annual), and much more
- Excellent Parental benefits including: 12-20 weeks of Paid
  Parental Leave, Carrot Fertility
  Benefits/Adoption/Surrogacy support, Back-up Child Care
  support
- Mental health and wellness benefits including no cost
  employee access to Modern Health coaching & therapy
  Sessions and high quality physician office experience via
  One Medical membership (select U.S. locations only)
- Employee Stock Purchase Program (ESPP)

Other fun facts about Amplitude:

- We were recognized in the Newsweek Excellence Index 2024.
- Our customers love us! They've said we're the #1 product
  analytics solution for 19 quarters in a row on G2.
- We care A LOT about product innovation. Fast Company called
  us the #3 most innovative enterprise company in the world.
- We invest in our people. We offer mentorship programs,
  management training, and wellness initiatives.
- We give back to our communities. We give every Ampliteer a
  charitable giving grant and paid volunteer time off.
- We were founded in 2012, went public via a direct listing in
  September 2021, and are now trading under the ticker
  $AMPL.
- We’re a global and fast-growing team! We have employees
  around the world and offices in San Francisco (HQ), New
  York, Vancouver, Amsterdam, London, Paris, Singapore, and
  Tokyo.
- Our mascot is Data Monster, who loves to chow down on
  numbers, charts, and graphs. Nom nom.

Amplitude provides equal employment opportunities (EEO). All
applicants are considered without regards to race, color,
religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status,
ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, or
sexual orientation.

This role is eligible for equity, benefits and other forms
of compensation.

Based on Colorado law, the following details are for
individuals who will work for Amplitude in Colorado.
Colorado range: $199,000 - $298,000 total target cash
(inclusive of bonus or commission)

Based on legislation in New York City, the following details
are for individuals who will work for Amplitude in New York
City. New York City salary range: $222,000 - $333,000 total
target cash (inclusive of bonus or commission)

Based on legislation in California, the following details
are for individuals who will work for Amplitude in San
Francisco Bay Area of California. Salary range: $222,000 -
$333,000 total target cash (inclusive of bonus or
commission)

Based on legislation in California, the following details
are for individuals who will work for Amplitude in
California outside of the San Francisco Bay Area. California
salary range: $199,000 - $298,000 total target cash
(inclusive of bonus or commission)

Based on legislation in Washington state, the following
details are for individuals who will work for Amplitude in
Washington state. Washington salary range: $199,000 -
$298,000 total target cash (inclusive of bonus or
commission)

Based on legislation in Washington state, the following
details are for individuals who will work for Amplitude in
Washington only: unlimited PTO, 10 to 13 holidays annually
(will vary), medical dental and vision PPO and CDHP plans.
Finally, a company sponsored 401(k) retirement plan.

By applying for this job, you acknowledge that Amplitude
processes your personal data in accordance with the
Amplitude Applicant Privacy Notice.

Staying Safe - Protect Yourself From Recruitment Fraud We
are aware of individuals and entities fraudulently
representing themselves as Amplitude recruiters and/or
hiring managers. Amplitude will never ask for financial
information or payment, or for personal information such as
bank account number or social security number during the job
application or interview process. Any emails from the
Amplitude recruiting team will come from an @amplitude.com
email address. You can learn more about how to protect
yourself from these types of fraud by referring to this
article. Please exercise caution and cease communications if
something feels suspicious about your interactions.
responsibilities: Shape the narrative for product development in the AI era by articulating why analytics becomes critical as AI accelerates building speed
Develop messaging that resonates with Product Managers while demonstrating technical depth to earn credibility with Engineering partners
Drive adoption by leading launches for experimentation, feature management, and LLM analytics capabilities focused on PM workflows
Build enablement programs for sales and customer success teams on experimentation culture and AI-readiness
Create compelling product stories and case studies with customer evidence showcasing how experimentation enables product velocity
Position Amplitude's unique value for product teams by differentiating from developer-first tools
Partner with product teams to shape roadmap based on how modern product teams want to work with AI tools
use_cases: AI model performance testing and measurement for product teams
Feature experimentation and A/B testing for AI-powered products
LLM analytics to track and evaluate large language model outputs in production
Feature flags and gradual rollouts for AI features to test models on small cohorts
Data-driven product development enabling PMs to validate hypotheses without engineering dependencies
Real-time user behavior and business outcome measurement for AI product optimization
ai_classification_reasoning:
This is a Product Marketing Manager role focused on go-to-
market strategy, positioning, and marketing content for
Amplitude's AI analytics platform. While the role requires
technical fluency with AI product development workflows and
LLM analytics, it does not involve building, training, fine-
tuning, or deploying AI models directly. The role exists to
market and position AI/analytics products that other teams
build.
